_comment: Baseline of KNOWN, PRE-EXISTING schema violations the strong gate tolerates
(xfail) so a main-branch fix to the validation GATE (issue #19) never reddens
CI on an exporter bug tracked separately. Each entry waives EXACTLY one
part+signature: a NEW violation (other part, other signature) still fails;
once the exporter is fixed, validate.py reports the entry as stale (non-fatal)
so it can be removed and the part is then validated genuinely.
The three original entries (DOCX raw wps:wsp text box/shape, XLSX inline <t>
xml:space, ODS table-row before table-column) were FIXED in the Office
exporter (2026-06-25, #19 follow-up): the DOCX exporter now emits
Transitional-native VML (w:pict / v:shape — wps:wsp and mc:AlternateContent
are both unschema'd in the ECMA-376 Transitional set and rejected by
a:graphicData's strict wildcard), the XLSX inline string emits a bare <t>
(CT_Rst/ST_Xstring allows no attributes), and the ODS table emits
<table:table-column> before its rows. All three parts now validate against
the official ECMA-376 XSD / OASIS RelaxNG schemas, so no waiver remains.
